posts.tweetText: this week saw a dozen founders with shipped products at zero revenue. the silence after deploy is heavier than the work before it. building is the easy part.
posts.basedOnPattern: Multiple founders with shipped products at $0, no users, silence after deploy

posts.tweetText: building something nobody knows about hits different at 2am. you keep shipping into the void hoping someone will notice. most days they don't.
posts.basedOnPattern: Multiple founders isolated building in silence, no users, stuck at $0

posts.tweetText: spending a lot of time lately looking at beauty business websites and i keep noticing the same thing. the salon is incredible in person and then you get to their page and it's like a completely different place. working on something about that
just swatched 6 opi shades on my hand during lunch and i have zero regrets. this is normal behavior
went down a rabbit hole looking at salon booking pages today and found the same 3 problems on almost every one. the fix is usually fast but nobody told them
if your salon has 200 five star reviews and a booking page that doesn't load on mobile you are losing clients every single day and probably don't know it

ran across thread where dental clinic cut no shows switching from calls to texts. evening reminders at 6 to 7 pm get better response than morning. people on phones in evening with time to think about tomorrow. morning reminders get buried in rush. makes sense more i think about
a salon without prices listed is not creating mystery. it's creating friction. put the prices up
been tracking which beauty businesses actually respond to comments on instagram vs which ones don't. the ones who do are not the biggest accounts. interesting
most beauty businesses don't have a visibility problem. they have a conversion problem. traffic is fine. the page loses them
there's a version of your business that shows up online exactly as good as the work you do in person. most aren't there yet. that gap is fixable
looked at 40 salon websites this week. about half had a booking button that went somewhere broken or outdated. half.
a pdf menu is not a service page. google can't read it. your phone clients can't open it. it helps no one. put the services on the actual website
a lot of beauty business owners are doing everything right inside the salon and nothing is working online. usually it is one or two specific things. not the whole page
there are two types of people. people who pick a nail color in 2 minutes and people who take 45 and then get the same one they always get. i am both depending on the day
the silence after you ship something real is heavier than you expect. months of work, zero users, and you're just talking to yourself. sometimes i wonder how many good ideas died in that quiet.
posts.basedOnPattern: Multiple founders building in silence with zero users/traction - isolation of shipping into the void
